Source_Species.reason: A. altissima is explicitly identified as tree of heaven.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper explicitly studies A. altissima (Ailanthus altissima).

Applied_Species.reason: Functional testing was performed by transient expression in N. benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Nicotiana benthamiana is explicitly named as the heterologous host.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: AaCYP88A154 forms 7,8-epoxymelianol, the shared precursor to isomeliandiol and protoglabretal; the paper also links this step to apoprotolimonoid/limonoid/quassinoid and glabretane biosynthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These products are explicitly discussed as triterpenoids.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper states these enzymes expand triterpenoid scaffold diversity and are crucial for production of limonoid, quassinoid, apoprotolimonoid, and glabretane triterpenoids.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The study is explicitly about plant triterpenoid biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The paper describes post-cyclization skeletal rearrangement of tirucallane/protolimonoid-type triterpenoids at a pathway bifurcation/branchpoint.

Enzyme_Name.reason: AaCYP88A154 is explicitly described as a cytochrome P450 monooxygenase.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The gene name and text identify it as CYP88A154, a cytochrome P450 monooxygenase in the CYP88 family.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Microsomes were used for in vitro assays after heterologous production, but the paper does not explicitly state native subcellular localization of AaCYP88A154.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Candidate genes were selected from a root-predominant co-expression cluster, and the paper states these pathway genes showed root-specific/high root expression.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The Results explicitly test and identify melianol (8) as the substrate consumed by AaCYP88A154.

Primary_Product.reason: Compound 9 was purified and identified as 7,8-epoxymelianol, produced by AaCYP88A154 from melianol.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper explicitly concludes that AaCYP88A154 epoxidizes the C-7,8 double bond of melianol to form 7,8-epoxymelianol.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: It is shown to act after melianol formation and before the ISM1/ISM2 rearrangement branch.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: 7,8-epoxymelianol is explicitly the immediate shared precursor for the two rearranged branches.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper calls it a central/key step enabling downstream pathways, but does not directly demonstrate rate limitation or use the exact term control point for this enzyme alone.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show melianol consumption and formation of 7,8-epoxymelianol enabling downstream isomeliandiol/protoglabretal production.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are directly supported: only this oxidoreductase candidate clearly consumed melianol, it generated 7,8-epoxymelianol, and the paper links this epoxidation to those triterpenoid classes.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are described: transient co-expression in N. benthamiana, LC-MS, purification/NMR, m-CPBA comparison, and yeast microsome assays.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: These genes were co-expressed in pathway reconstruction, but direct protein-protein interactions are not shown.

Omics_Data.reason: The paper explicitly used RNA-seq/de novo transcriptome expression and SOM co-expression analysis in A. altissima; no accession is given in the provided text.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Both heterologous expression in N. benthamiana and chemical epoxidation with m-CPBA are explicitly described.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The abstract and conclusions explicitly state the enzymes are crucial for biotechnological production of those triterpenoid classes.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The text explicitly states the products/intermediate are acid-labile and notes close homologues in Citrus sinensis and Citrus grandis.

Source_Species.reason: A. altissima is explicitly identified as tree of heaven.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The source organism is Ailanthus altissima.

Applied_Species.reason: AaISM1 was tested by co-expression in N. benthamiana.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: AaISM1 shifts the pathway strongly toward isomeliandiol and the paper links ISM1 to the apoprotolimonoid branch and related limonoid/quassinoid biosynthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Isomeliandiol and related products are discussed as triterpenoids.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The study explicitly frames ISM1 as expanding/switching triterpenoid scaffold diversity.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The pathway is plant triterpenoid biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: ISM1 is explicitly assigned to the apoprotolimonoid-forming rearrangement branch.

Enzyme_Name.reason: ISM1 is explicitly named as one of the isomerases.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper states ISM1 is related to C-8,7 sterol isomerases from primary metabolism.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Microsomal assays were performed, but native subcellular localization of ISM1 is not explicitly stated.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: ISM candidates came from the root-predominant co-expression cluster; ISM1 and ISM2 are said to have highly similar expression profiles to pathway genes.

Primary_Substrate.reason: ISM1 was tested in the presence of the shared epoxide substrate 7,8-epoxymelianol and is described as using that substrate.

Primary_Product.reason: Co-expression with ISM1 caused a strong shift toward isomeliandiol (10) as the major product.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper states ISM1 channels the rearrangement of 7,8-epoxymelianol toward isomeliandiol via skeletal rearrangement.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: ISM1 acts downstream of AaCYP88A154 at the branchpoint rearrangement step.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: It directly forms the branch-defining rearranged scaffold/product isomeliandiol.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper explicitly calls ISM1 and ISM2 central gatekeepers controlling branch formation.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Figure 4A and text state ISM1 causes a strong shift toward isomeliandiol formation.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are directly aligned with Results and conclusions.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Transient co-expression, LC-MS profiling, and yeast microsome assays are explicitly described.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper shows pathway co-function with AaCYP88A154 and comparison to AaISM2, but not direct physical interactions.

Omics_Data.reason: ISM1 was selected from RNA-seq/SOM co-expression analysis of A. altissima transcripts; no accession is provided.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Heterologous expression in N. benthamiana is explicitly used for validation.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper states these enzymes are crucial for biotechnological production of related triterpenoid classes, supporting engineering relevance.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly states ISM1 evolved from sterol isomerases of primary metabolism.

Source_Species.reason: A. altissima is explicitly identified as tree of heaven.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The source organism is Ailanthus altissima.

Applied_Species.reason: AaISM2 was functionally tested in N. benthamiana.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: AaISM2 produces protoglabretal and is linked to the glabretane triterpenoid branch.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Protoglabretal and glabretanes are described as triterpenoids.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper explicitly presents ISM2 as contributing to triterpenoid scaffold diversification.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The study concerns plant triterpenoid biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: ISM2 is explicitly concluded to control formation of the glabretane structural subclass.

Enzyme_Name.reason: ISM2 is explicitly named as an isomerase candidate/productive enzyme.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper states ISM2 is related to C-8,7 sterol isomerases.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Microsomal assays were used, but native localization of ISM2 is not explicitly reported.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: ISM2 came from the root-associated co-expression cluster and is said to have a highly similar expression profile to ISM1.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The paper explicitly states ISM2 accepted only 7,8-epoxymelianol (9) as substrate, not isomeliandiol (10).

Primary_Product.reason: The new product formed by ISM2 was isolated and identified as protoglabretal (11).

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results show ISM2 catalyzes skeletal rearrangement of 7,8-epoxymelianol to cyclopropane-containing protoglabretal.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: ISM2 acts downstream of the epoxide intermediate at the branchpoint rearrangement step.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: It directly forms the branch-defining rearranged scaffold/product for the glabretane branch.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper explicitly calls ISM1 and ISM2 central gatekeepers controlling structural subclass formation.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Text states ISM2 caused almost complete disappearance of epoxide 9 and formation of new product 11.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are directly supported by Figure 4/text and the in vitro substrate specificity assay.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Transient co-expression, LC-MS, purification/NMR, yeast microsome assays, and substrate specificity testing are all explicitly described.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Pathway co-expression with AaCYP88A154 and comparison with AaISM1 is shown, but not direct protein interactions.

Omics_Data.reason: ISM2 was identified from A. altissima RNA-seq/SOM co-expression analysis; no accession is provided.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Heterologous expression in N. benthamiana is explicitly used.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper argues these genes are crucial for biotechnology and highlights ISM2 as enabling a novel cyclopropane-containing scaffold.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly states ISM2 represents a new route for cyclopropane installation and that ISM genes evolved from sterol isomerases.
